title: NSDR / Yoga Nidra
blurb: Deep guided rest that restores without sleep.
lead: NSDR (non-sleep deep rest) and its root practice Yoga Nidra guide you into deep relaxation while staying awake — restorative and calming.

blocks.text: Yoga Nidra is a guided practice of progressive relaxation done lying down. 'NSDR' is a modern, secular term for the same kind of deep-rest protocol.
It calms the nervous system, can reduce stress and improve sleep, and offers restoration when you're depleted but can't nap. Evidence is promising and still developing.

items: Lie down comfortably on your back, warm and supported.
Follow a guided recording (below) — you don't need to do anything but listen.
The guide leads attention through the body, breath, and sensations.
Let yourself drift into deep rest while staying just awake.
There's no goal to achieve — receiving the rest is enough.

blocks.text: Worrying about falling asleep. Drifting off occasionally is fine — the practice still restores you.

resources.title: Insight Timer
resources.whyHere: Free library of Yoga Nidra and NSDR practices.
